title: beckmannia eruciformis subsp. baicalensis
intro: Beckmannia eruciformis subsp. baicalensis, also known as Baikal jointgrass or Baicalian joint-grass, is a subspecies of the plant Beckmannia eruciformis that belongs to the family Poaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Baikal jointgrass include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Baikal jointgrass is typically prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional folk medicine to treat various ailments.
